question.text: Under SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, what must an intermediary do with its certificate of registration?

answer.text: Display the certificate only at the registered office
Keep the original certificate locked in a safe at the head office only
Submit the original certificate to the stock exchange for safekeeping
Prominently display a photocopy of the certificate at all its offices including branch offices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The regulation requires each intermediary to prominently display a photocopy of the certificate at all its offices including branch offices. **The Trap:** Displaying only at the registered office seems reasonable but the requirement extends to all offices including branches. **Cross-Exam Context:** Registration certificate display requirements are tested in NISM Series III-B and Series V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure every branch office has a visible copy of the registration certificate - inspectors check this during routine inspections.
